chunks.text: EECS Cal Day - EECS at Berkeley. EECS students, faculty, and staff host a variety of events on Cal Day to introduce newly admitted students and their families to our community. Cal Day 2026 will be dedicated to registered, newly admitted students and their families only. Saturday, April 18, 2026.
chunks.question: What is the purpose of EECS Cal Day?
chunks.answer: Introduce newly admitted students to the community.

chunks.text: Cal Day In-Person Events: EECS Advisor Tabling in the Engineering Information Zone. Meet your Electrical Engineering & Computer Science Advisors! They’re here to help you as you navigate your journey through this amazing degree program. Ask questions about requirements and how to get involved as a student.
chunks.question: Where can students meet EECS advisors on Cal Day?
chunks.answer: At the Engineering Information Zone tabling.

chunks.text: CS Advisor Tabling in the Northwest Information Zone. Meet your Computer Science Advisors! They’re here to help you as you navigate your journey through this amazing degree program. Ask questions about requirements and how to get involved as a student.
chunks.question: What can students ask CS advisors about?

chunks.text: Student Sessions: Tours of Soda and Cory Halls depart from the 3rd floor lobby in Soda Hall every 30 minutes. Kickstarting Your Journey as a Woman in CS! Find your community of women interested in computer science, learn about a free EECS-sponsored residential CS bootcamp with an Amazon Field Trip, and make bracelets while chatting with alumni.
chunks.question: What activities are included for women in CS?
chunks.answer: Community building, bootcamp info, and bracelet making.

chunks.text: Women in Tech Panel and Lunch with AWE. The Association of Women in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (AWE) invites you to a panel on Cal Day! Here you can learn about what it’s like to be a Berkeley CS/EECS student, learn about our organization, find out about opportunities, and get all your questions answered with lunch provided.
chunks.question: What does the AWE panel offer attendees?
chunks.answer: Student insights, organization info, and lunch.

chunks.text: Computer Science Mentors: Intro to the Berkeley EE/CS Community. Computer Science Mentors (CSM) is a student-run organization that provides small group tutoring sessions to help students gain a deeper understanding of concepts and develop a feeling of community. Join us as we introduce the EE/CS program at Berkeley – how to navigate lower-division courses, how to find a welcoming community, etc.
chunks.question: What does CSM provide to students?
chunks.answer: Small group tutoring and community building.

chunks.text: Welcome to Machine Learning at Berkeley! Join us for an intro to Machine Learning at Berkeley, some advice on being a Berkeley engineer, and a panel with current students pursuing machine learning and AI! The Art of Creating an Animated Film: In this event we will be showing you how we create an animated short film in an hour going through the whole pipeline using Autodesk Maya!
chunks.question: What is covered in the Machine Learning event?
chunks.answer: Intro to ML, engineering advice, and student panel.

chunks.text: Computer Science Upper-Division Showcase: This presentation will provide you an overview of the upper-division Computer Science course offerings at Berkeley, which will make up the bulk of your academic experience. The talk will also cover graduate coursework, obtaining research positions, the job search, student organization leadership, and common non-CS electives.
chunks.question: What does the Upper-Division Showcase include?
chunks.answer: Course overviews, research, jobs, and electives.

chunks.text: Student Group Tabling: Meet some of the amazing student organizations from Electrical Engineering and Computer Science! Participating student organizations: CS Mentors, CS Kickstart, Computer Science Undergraduate Association, Association of Women in EE&CS, Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) EECS Honors Society, UC Berkeley Undergraduate Graphics Group (UCBUGG), Machine Learning at Berkeley (ML@B), EECS/CS Student Organization Tabling, Pioneers in Engineering (PiE), Hispanic Engineers and Scientists.
chunks.question: Which student organizations are tabling?
chunks.answer: CS Mentors, AWE, HKN, ML@B, PiE, and others.

chunks.text: Faculty Presentations: The Beauty and the Joy of Binary Numbers. We will teach you some computer science through an interactive example, a magic trick, and show you the secret behind a very famous game so you’ll never lose again! CS Major Information Session: Join us for an engaging presentation with the Computer Science Division in the EECS Department, where you will learn about the department, academics, and exciting enrichment opportunities!
chunks.question: What is taught in the Binary Numbers presentation?
chunks.answer: CS through examples, tricks, and game secrets.

chunks.text: EECS Major Information Session: Join us for an engaging presentation with the EECS Department, where you will learn about the department, academics, and exciting enrichment opportunities! Student Demonstrations: GamesCrafters is a computational game theory research and development group led by Teaching Professor Dan Garcia. Our GAMESMAN system strongly solves two-person, abstract strategy games and one-person puzzles for play and analysis.
chunks.question: What does GamesCrafters demonstrate?
chunks.answer: Game theory solutions for strategy games and puzzles.

chunks.text: IEEE Soldering Workshop: Join us for an exciting hands-on workshop hosted by our IEEE Student Branch, where you’ll learn how to solder a PCB (Printed Circuit Board)! Whether you’re completely new or simply looking to sharpen your skills, this workshop is designed for beginners with no prior experience required. Assemble a simple PCB that you can take home!
chunks.question: What do participants do in the soldering workshop?
chunks.answer: Learn to solder and assemble a take-home PCB.

chunks.text: PiE Robotics Demonstration: Pioneers in Engineering (PiE) is a non-profit organization that provides a quality STEM learning experience for students in underserved Bay Area high schools. We run outreach workshops in the Fall and an annual 8-week robotics competition in the Spring. Come drive some of our staff-built robots and talk to PiE staff about how we develop our kits!
chunks.question: What does PiE offer during Cal Day?
chunks.answer: Robot driving and kit development discussions.

chunks.text: EECS Student Panel: EE&CS Student Life Panel. Join our student panelists to learn more about campus life as an Electrical Engineering or Computer Science student! From academics and clubs, to research and professional experiences, get a glimpse into what your future could be here at Cal. Accessibility: If you require an accommodation for effective communication or information about campus mobility access features in order to fully participate in our virtual events, please contact Grayson Johnston (gjohnston@berkeley.edu) with as much advance notice as possible and at least 7 days in advance of the event.
chunks.question: What topics does the student panel cover?
chunks.answer: Academics, clubs, research, and professional experiences.
